name: Weggeliteig (Button Rolls)
recipeIngredient: 1 Egg, beaten with a pinch of salt
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease a large baking sheet or have a Teflon-coated one ready.
2. In a large mixing bowl, pour the hot water and add the milk, sugar, salt, and 2 cups flour. Stir to blend.
3. Add the yeast and stir again. Gradually incorporate the shortening.
4. The Swiss baker who created this recipe insists that half of the shortening should be lard for authentic flavor.
5. If using a wooden spoon, beat until combined; if using a mixer with a flat beater, mix until smooth.
6. Add the remaining flour, about 1/2 cup at a time, until the dough is shaggy and can be worked by hand or under the dough hook.
7. Knead for 8 minutes by hand or in the mixer until the dough is soft and elastic.
8. Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover tightly with plastic wrap, and let rise until doubled in size, about 2 hours.
9. Turn the dough out of the bowl and gently punch down.
10. Divide the dough into 24 pieces, roll each into a ball, and then shape it into long rolls (4-5 inches) by pressing back and forth under a flattened palm.
11. Arrange the rolls on the baking sheet with about 3 inches between rows. If there isn't enough space for all rolls, reserve some dough and repeat this step when the sheet and oven are ready.
12. Cover the rolls with wax paper and let rest for 40 minutes.
13. Brush each roll with the egg/salt mixture, then leave uncovered for another 20 minutes.
14. Brush the rolls again with the glaze before baking.
15. Preheat the oven to 375F.
16. When ready, cut 5 triangular slits (about 1 inch long) across each roll from center to edge using a sharp pair of scissors dipped in water frequently to prevent sticking.
17. Halfway through baking, turn the sheet end for end to ensure even cooking. The rolls are done when they are glossy and brown, about 35 minutes.
18. Gently check that the crust is firm by tapping it with your finger.
19. Serve on a rack, allowing the rolls to cool slightly before eating.
